source.title: BSACI guideline for the diagnosis and management of allergic and non-allergic rhinitis (Revised Edition 2017; First edition 2007)
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE allergy, ENT, prescribing, and referral adaptation
source.population: Children and adults with suspected or established allergic, non-allergic, occupational, or other rhinitis phenotypes, subject to section-specific qualifiers.
source.setting: Primary, allergy, respiratory, paediatric, and ENT assessment and review.
source.exclusions: Exact for rhinitis history, triggers, nasal and eye symptoms, sleep and mouth-breathing impact, medicine and social context, nasal examination, septum, secretions, crusting, polyps, postnasal drip, and clinician-selected testing context. It does not diagnose a subtype or generate treatment.

exact_location.heading: Sections 7.1–7.10 — history, triggers, symptom pattern, impact, and contextual factors

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting onset, duration, triggers, rhinorrhoea, obstruction, sneezing, itching, crusting, eye and lower-airway symptoms, sleep, snoring, mouth breathing, family, social, occupational, and medicine context without assigning rhinitis subtype.

items.final_wording: Supports recording clinician-selected allergy testing and already available specialist or imaging results; routine radiology and autonomous testing are not mapped.

source.title: BSACI guideline for the diagnosis and management of allergic and non-allergic rhinitis (Revised Edition 2017; First edition 2007)
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE allergy, ENT, prescribing, and referral adaptation
source.population: Children and adults with suspected or established allergic, non-allergic, occupational, or other rhinitis phenotypes, subject to section-specific qualifiers.
source.setting: Primary, allergy, respiratory, paediatric, and ENT assessment and review.
source.exclusions: Exact for rhinitis history, triggers, nasal and eye symptoms, sleep and mouth-breathing impact, medicine and social context, nasal examination, septum, secretions, crusting, polyps, postnasal drip, and clinician-selected testing context. It does not diagnose a subtype or generate treatment.

exact_location.heading: Sections 7.1–7.10 — history, triggers, symptom pattern, impact, and contextual factors

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting onset, duration, triggers, rhinorrhoea, obstruction, sneezing, itching, crusting, eye and lower-airway symptoms, sleep, snoring, mouth breathing, family, social, occupational, and medicine context without assigning rhinitis subtype.

added_items.rationale: The exact rhinitis history section explicitly supports crusting, discharge, obstruction, bleeding and contextual symptom documentation, including onset, impact, associated features, and relevant negatives, without assigning a cause.

added_items.final_wording: Supports clinician-performed general inspection, mouth-breathing observation, airflow assessment, anterior rhinoscopy, secretions, septal appearance, polyps, crusting, throat, and postnasal-drip documentation.

source.title: BSACI guideline for the diagnosis and management of allergic and non-allergic rhinitis (Revised Edition 2017; First edition 2007)
source.jurisdiction: United Kingdom specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE allergy, ENT, prescribing, and referral adaptation
source.population: Children and adults with suspected or established allergic, non-allergic, occupational, or other rhinitis phenotypes, subject to section-specific qualifiers.
source.setting: Primary, allergy, respiratory, paediatric, and ENT assessment and review.
source.exclusions: Exact for rhinitis history, triggers, nasal and eye symptoms, sleep and mouth-breathing impact, medicine and social context, nasal examination, septum, secretions, crusting, polyps, postnasal drip, and clinician-selected testing context. It does not diagnose a subtype or generate treatment.

exact_location.heading: Sections 8.1–8.3 — visual, nasal, oral, and upper-airway examination

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-performed general inspection, mouth-breathing observation, airflow assessment, anterior rhinoscopy, secretions, septal appearance, polyps, crusting, throat, and postnasal-drip documentation.

added_items.rationale: The exact examination section supports clinician-performed general, nasal, septal, secretion, crusting, polyp, and throat assessment without auto-populating findings.

added_items.final_wording: Supports recording clinician-selected allergy testing and already available specialist or imaging results; routine radiology and autonomous testing are not mapped.

exact_location.heading: Sections 9.1–9.8 — allergy testing and investigation context

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording clinician-selected allergy testing and already available specialist or imaging results; routine radiology and autonomous testing are not mapped.

added_items.rationale: The exact investigation context supports review of existing specialist information and a clinician-entered plan or follow-up without recommending tests, topical products, or referral.
